text.title: AI-Generated Test Validation Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped ai-generated-test-validation work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Test asserts price = qty * unit by re-implementing the same multiplication; passes even when the function uses + instead of *.
antipattern.root-cause: AI was prompted with the implementation; emitted a translation, not a specification.
antipattern.fix: Replace assertion with known-input/known-output pairs (table-driven test). See rule no-mirror-test.

antipattern.symptom: Coverage report shows 92% lines; defects still land in production at the same rate.
antipattern.root-cause: Tests execute code paths without checking results.
antipattern.fix: Require at least one behavioural assert() per test; AST-check via static rule. See rule behavioural-assertion.

antipattern.symptom: test_orders_creates_order mocks Order.create then asserts Order.create was called; production bug in Order.create passes.
antipattern.root-cause: Author confused unit under test with collaborators.
antipattern.fix: Static AST check: any mock target that overlaps with the file-under-test path is rejected. See rule no-mock-of-sut.

antipattern.symptom: Test asserts result is not None but production code is broken in 5 distinct ways and still returns non-None.
antipattern.root-cause: Loose assertion left in after copy-paste.
antipattern.fix: Run mutation-testing; any test that survives every mutation is rejected. See rule assertion-must-fail-once.
